title: podman freezes during build and run commands
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**


Podman freezes randomly during build and pull commands, which are executed from jenkins server. Afterwards its not possible to execute any command of podman. It will stuck in CLI until the server gets restarted completely. Furthermore i'am not able to kill the podman process by their PID and if check all running processes by htop, i can see, that about 30 "**storage-chown-by-maps**" commands are pending for a rootless owned overlay. 

I already tried to remove the pending overlay folders by sudo and by executing podman / buildah unshare -> rm -rf *overlay* which also freezed.

I checked the kernel log and found the following message:
**overlayfs: conflicting options: userxattr, metacopy=on** 

We're running all of our containers in rootless mode and using the fuse-overlay storage layer for this. 


**Describe the results you received:**

Podman freezes

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version

API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.17.5

```

comments.body:
I don't see fuse-overlays being used from your configuration.

Are you sure these processes are stuck or just slow?

Could you strace them to see where they are stuck?

> I don't see fuse-overlays being used from your configuration.

Doesn't graphDriverName: overlay means that we're running on fuse-overlay ? 

> Are you sure these processes are stuck or just slow?

Waited for more than 2 Hours. Unfortunately it happend already multiple times. Also for smaller builds which usually take ~ 2 minutes.  

> Could you strace them to see where they are stuck?

Sure. Currently it is running fine again. I will send send you the tracereport next time.

> > I don't see fuse-overlays being used from your configuration.
> 
> Doesn't graphDriverName: overlay means that we're running on fuse-overlay ?


no, it must show a mountProgram as well, otherwise it is using native overlay.

> > > I don't see fuse-overlays being used from your configuration.
> > 
> > 
> > Doesn't graphDriverName: overlay means that we're running on fuse-overlay ?
> 
> no, it must show a mountProgram as well, otherwise it is using native overlay.

Oh thank ! You're right. I used an old storage.conf file.

Now its configured like this:

`graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-1.8.2-1.el9.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.10.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.8.2
        FUSE library version 3.10.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing since this seems like it is solved.
